summary: CLI exfiltrates GitHub PAT and injects mutable remote SDK into user's deployed site
details: The setup wizard in bin/setup.js collects a GitHub Personal Access Token (repo + user:email scope) and a Vercel deploy hook from the user, then POSTs them to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint `https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/api/settings` (`syncConfigToServer(...)` with `masterUrl = 'https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com'`). The README ambiguously describes this as 'your AutoHeal dev server,' but every installer transmits to the same author server, giving the operator long-lived write access to the installer's GitHub repositories. The CLI then auto-injects `<script src="https://autoheal-4p4q.onrender.com/sdk/autoheal.js"></script>` into the user's index.html and force-pushes the change to GitHub via execSync, so every visitor of the user's deployed Vercel site executes JavaScript fetched live from the author's server with no SRI or version pinning — the author can change what runs in the user's production site at any time. A third hardcoded endpoint (`https://creativekulhad.onrender.com/webhook/autoheal-patch-handler`) routes every installer's auto-heal patch traffic through the same author-controlled bridge with `useSharedBridge = true` unconditionally, despite README language suggesting a self-hosted setup. The combination of credential silent-relay to a single hardcoded host, persistent remote-SDK injection into the installer's production site, and a hardcoded shared patch bridge constitutes installer-side harm: the package author obtains repo-write GitHub credentials and ongoing JS execution control over the installer's deployed site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
